destinations.evidence: Legacy verification maps read_scheduled_call errors or None to CallNotFound before later checks.
Block-building filtering emits C1CallNotFound when batch_read_scheduled_calls lacks the call id.
destinations.snippet: let call = match calls_map.get(&call_id) { Some(c) => c, None => { results.push(FilterResult::C1CallNotFound); continue; } };

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port: missing Scheduler calls are rejected as CallNotFound/C1 before downstream validation.
rationale: The current legacy scheduled verifier maps missing or unreadable scheduled call storage to CallNotFound before later state, timing, or execution checks.
statement: Scheduled transaction verification must reject a scheduled transaction whose call_id cannot be read from Scheduler contract storage, returning ScheduledConstraintViolation::CallNotFound for that call id.
suggested_falsification_tests: Verify a scheduled tx with absent call_id and assert ScheduledConstraintViolation::CallNotFound.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Existing C1 call-not-found tests should remain part of scheduled verifier coverage.
title: Scheduled verification rejects missing calls
